description:
A cognitive psychology lab has collected continuous-response Visual Working Memory (VWM) data using a color wheel paradigm. Analyze the data in ~/pebl/data/color_wheel_data.csv (containing participant_id, trial, set_size, target_color, response_color, and rt_ms) to compute circular error metrics for each participant and set size.

Calculate the signed angular error as the shortest distance around the 360-degree circle from target to response (ranging from -180 to 180) and the absolute angular error. For each participant and set size, determine the Mean Absolute Error (mae), the guess rate (proportion of trials where absolute angular error is strictly greater than 60 degrees), and the population standard deviation of signed angular errors for trials where the absolute angular error is <= 60 degrees (precision_sd). If a participant has no trials within the precision range for a given set size, record the precision_sd as null.

Identify and exclude participants whose overall guess rate across all set sizes combined is strictly greater than 0.50. Export a JSON report to ~/pebl/analysis/vwm_report.json containing the metrics for each participant (including exclusion status and reason for those excluded) and the group means for each metric per set size calculated across all non-excluded participants.
